description:
# Why

`check_legacy_worktree` (src/doctor.rs) emits:

    state worktree at .balls/worktree is not a valid linked git worktree
    hint: run `bl repair` to rebuild the state worktree

But `cmd_repair` (src/commands/repair.rs) only does three things: forget half-pushes, scan task JSON files, and (with `--fix`) clean up orphan claims/worktrees. It does *not* rebuild the linked git worktree at `.balls/worktree/`. The hint is pre-existing — predates the master_url split — and the master_url-mode hint that bl-c61b just landed correctly points at `bl prime` for re-materialization.

Doctor's whole job is naming the right command. Pointing users at one that won't fix the problem defeats it.

# Concrete fix

Pick the actually-corrective path and name it:
- if `setup_state_branch` is callable as a re-materialize for the legacy layout, surface that
- otherwise the operational fix is `rm -rf .balls/worktree && bl init` (or a new `bl repair` arm that adds linked-worktree rebuild — would justify the existing hint)

# Out of scope

Master_url branch (`check_state_repo`) — bl-c61b already names the right command there. Only the legacy branch is wrong.

# Origin

Flagged during bl-c61b review pass.
title: bl doctor: legacy 'state worktree' hint points at bl repair, which doesn't rebuild it
